info.title: StateSmith.Cli
info.description: Command line tools for StateSmith - a cross platform, free/open source tool for generating state machines in multiple languages. The generated code is human readable, has zero dependencies and is suitable for use with tiny bare metal microcontrollers, kernels, computers... It avoids dynamic memory allocations for the safety or performance inclined.

commands.name: create
commands.description: Create a new StateSmith project from template.

options.description: Specifies programming language for transpiler. Ignored for csx files.

options.name: --dump-errors-to-file
options.description: Useful for troubleshooting. Exception stack traces will be written to file. Ignored if 'propagate-exceptions' is set. Ignored for .csx files.

options.name: --here
options.description: Runs code generation in this directory.

options.name: --include
options.description: Glob patterns to include. ex: `**/src/*.csx`. Can't use with -r.

options.name: --no-ask
options.description: Prevents tool from prompting you. Good for CI/CD.

options.name: --no-csx
options.description: Disables running csx files (useful if dotnet-script is not installed).

options.name: --no-sim-gen
options.description: Disables simulation .html file generation. Ignored for csx files.

options.name: --propagate-exceptions
options.description: Useful for troubleshooting. Exceptions will propagate out of SmRunner with original stack trace instead of being summarized and printed. Ignored for .csx files.

options.name: --rebuild
options.description: Ensures code generation is run. Ignores change detection.

arguments.name: Optional specific files
arguments.description: Use when you want to target specific files without directory scanning.

commands.name: setup
commands.description: Set up vscode for StateSmith & csx files.

options.name: --vscode-csx
options.description: Set up vscode for C# script debugging and intellisense.

options.name: --vscode-drawio-plugin
options.description: Set up vscode drawio extension with StateSmith plugin.
